name: Slab API
what_it_does: Team knowledge base platform with REST API for creating, organizing, and searching documentation with built-in verification workflows and deep integrations with Slack and GitHub.
use_cases: Searching internal documentation from agent context to answer team questions
Creating post-incident runbooks and retrospective documents programmatically
Querying post content and metadata for knowledge graph construction
Triggering documentation updates when code or process changes are detected
Building internal chatbots that surface relevant Slab posts as context
not_for: Real-time collaborative editing (use Notion or Google Docs instead)
best_when: An agent needs to retrieve or create structured team knowledge from a clean, well-organized company wiki.
avoid_when: You need a free tier for budget-constrained projects or extensive API automation beyond basic CRUD.

auth.notes: API tokens created per user from settings. Tokens inherit the user's permissions. No service account concept — agents typically use a dedicated team member's token.

pricing.notes: Billing is annual. No free tier makes it harder to evaluate for agent integration before committing.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; standard API rate limiting applies

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API is straightforward but documentation is limited. Error responses are JSON with human-readable messages. Search API supports full-text queries suitable for agent retrieval.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Post creation does not natively support idempotency keys. Check for existing posts by title or slug before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation is minimal — primary integration is via webhooks and Slack/GitHub apps
No rate limit documentation; conservative approach required
Post content is returned as HTML — agents need parsing to extract plain text
Verification status of posts is important for knowledge quality; unverified posts may be stale

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. US data residency only. User token model without service accounts is a security gap for agent deployments.
